market_notes: April 16, 2026: S&P 500 and Nasdaq at back-to-back record highs. TSM Q1 profit +58%, beats estimates, guides UP — AI foundry thesis confirmed. AMD closed at all-time high (longest winning streak since 2005). ORCL +29.4% 1W on AWS multicloud deal expansion. AVGO on 8-day winning streak. ZS short stopped out at $134.33 for -9.9% loss — software sector more resilient than expected. Iran peace talks continuing to support risk-on. Dense earnings cluster April 22-30: NOW, TSLA, VRT, GEV, then GOOG/META/MSFT/AMZN/NVDA results will be critical read-throughs. Portfolio at +4.6% total P&L with 13 open long positions, no shorts currently.
watching: TSM post-earnings gap — expecting +5-10% open April 17 on 58% profit surge and upward guidance; watch for follow-through or fade
NEM earnings April 23 — holding 15 shares at -0.3% unrealized; gold -4.7% 1W as Iran peace progresses; stop at $104.69; watching whether earnings beat revives gold miner thesis

NOW April 22 earnings — covered our short for +0.8% gain; watching whether NOW bounces further (+7.4% 1W) or AI disruption narrative reasserts; potential re-short post-earnings if guidance disappoints
TSLA April 22 — not held; watching for read-through on EV demand and energy/robotaxi narrative
VRT April 22 — AI data center cooling infrastructure; watching for AI capex confirmation
GEV April 22 — grid/power infrastructure; watching for energy demand read-through
GOOG/META/MSFT/AMZN April 29 — AI capex commentary will drive semiconductor sector direction
AMD at all-time high — 15 shares at +26.4% unrealized; stop at $202.57; watching for continuation or reversal at ATH
MRVL +33.5% unrealized — Stifel raised PT to $140; Nvidia expanding AI role; watching whether $140 target is hit and whether to take profits
ORCL +9.4% and +29.4% 1W on AWS deal — watching for continuation of re-rating from -48% below 52W high
SCHW reports April 16 — 2.72x vol ratio (highest in watchlist); watching for financial sector catalyst
INTC +55.5% 1M near 52W high — potential swing opportunity if AI foundry narrative extends

recent_runs.reasoning: **Macro:** Market tone has shifted decisively bearish on the Iran variable — Trump has issued warnings of hitting Iran 'hard' rather than de-escalation, reversing the risk-on narrative from last week. Oil has spiked sharply, and chip stocks are falling (headline: 'Chip Stocks Fall After Trump Iran Warning'). This is a significant macro headwind that changes my positioning calculus. SPY +1.7% 1W, QQQ +1.9% 1W, SMH +3.0% 1W show the week is still positive, but the Iran escalation news is fresh and creating volatility. US lawmakers proposing crackdown on chip tool sales to China is another semiconductor headwind affecting AMAT, LRCX, ASML specifically. Trump also just slapped 100% tariffs on some drug imports per WSJ/Barrons — major pharma headwind. **Sectors:** Semiconductors facing dual headwinds (Iran risk + China export crackdown news). Energy re-surging on Iran war fears — oil spiking. Optical networking (CIEN +15.4% 1W, LITE +20.1% 1W) remains explosive. Gold continuing to perform well (NEM +14.8% 1W). Banks (GS, JPM, C) still intact ahead of earnings April 13-16 but BofA warns retail brokers face lower engagement from Middle East conflict. Defense names mixed but RTX/NOC getting attention. **Positions:** Portfolio has 10 positions, ~42% deployed with ~$58.6K cash. Key concerns: (1) AMAT is directly in crosshairs of China chip tool export crackdown — this is a material risk to my thesis; AMAT -2% revenue growth and fwd P/E 25x doesn't justify holding through regulatory headwinds. (2) ASML similarly exposed to China restriction headlines; however ASML earnings April 15 provides a catalyst to hold for. (3) TSEM has had a parabolic move (+15.1% unrealized, +60.2% 1M) and Jim Cramer specifically called for taking profits; GlobalFoundries patent suit is a lingering risk. With Iran escalation reversing the de-escalation thesis that supported risk-on deployment, I need to be more selective. (4) ARM is down -3.7% 1W now, having given back some gains; still +20.1% 1M. (5) MRVL is the strongest position at +7.3% with NVIDIA $2B endorsement intact — hold. (6) MU flat but thesis intact on HBM supercycle; Mizuho says selloff overdone. (7) TSM reporting April 16 — hold pre-earnings as expected beat. (8) NEM +14.8% 1W — gold is the best performer, hold with stop. (9) GS reporting April 13 — hold into earnings. (10) DOCN +4.9% but 51.6x fwd P/E with no near-term catalyst — weakest fundamental case. Actions: (a) SELL DOCN — weakest fundamental thesis (51.6x fwd P/E, limited near-term catalyst, Iran/macro uncertainty reduces risk appetite for speculative names); (b) SELL AMAT — China chip tool export crackdown is a direct fundamental headwind; -2% revenue growth already weak; (c) TRIM TSEM — take partial profits on 15.1% gain, parabolic move flagged by Cramer, patent litigation risk, keep 7 shares to maintain exposure; (d) ADD to CIEN or LITE — optical networking exploding with +15.4%/+20.1% 1W; CIEN at -1.2% from 52W high, earnings not near-term, AI infrastructure buildout thesis compelling; however LITE already up 20.1% in a week and 108% YTD — too extended. CIEN at fwd P/E 54.2x with +33% revenue growth and near 52W highs is interesting but expensive and already moved a lot; I'll pass for now and watch for pullback. (e) Consider adding defense exposure given Iran war escalation — RTX or NOC. RTX +1.7% 1W, fwd P/E 26.1x with 12% revenue growth and Iran war directly fueling defense contracts. Analysts are divided but Barrons notes 'Iran War Fuels Defense Contracts.' I'll add a small RTX position. (f) Consider energy play — oil spiking on Iran war; LNG gets Wall Street upgrade 'as Iran War Reshapes Global LNG Demand'; LNG -3.5% 1W but +12.9% 1M with fwd P/E 15.7x and strong LNG demand thesis. The long-term agent holds LNG at -7.1% — my thesis diverges (short-term Iran oil spike catalyst). I'll add a small LNG position. Net result: Reduce 3 positions (close DOCN, close AMAT, trim TSEM), add 2 new positions (RTX small, LNG small). Portfolio goes from 10 to 9 positions post-actions, with ~$58K+ cash base plus proceeds from sales. Cash management: After selling DOCN (~$2,250), AMAT (~$4,182), trimming TSEM sells 8 shares (~$1,582), total proceeds ~$8,014. After buying RTX 5 shares (~$981) and LNG 5 shares (~$1,406), net cash increases by ~$5,627. Final cash ~$64,243 / portfolio value ~$100,599, cash ~63.9%. This is elevated but justified given: Iran escalation reversal of risk-on thesis, China chip export headwinds hitting key holdings, bank earnings about to drop as major binary events. Cash is strategic dry powder for post-earnings deployment.

**Macro:** The macro regime is volatile with Iran war escalation as the primary swing factor — oil spiking, defense benefiting, but chip stocks under pressure from both Iran uncertainty and China export crackdown legislation. Benchmarks show SPY +1.7% 1W, QQQ +1.9% 1W, SMH +3.0% 1W — the week was positive but the Iran escalation is fresh. Strong jobs data out today is pushing back Fed cut expectations (bonds falling), which is a headwind for rate-sensitive growth names. Bank earnings begin Monday (GS April 13, JPM/C/JNJ April 14) — these are major binary events. **Sectors:** Optical networking (CIEN +15.4% 1W, LITE +20.1% 1W) remains explosive — both near 52W highs, AI infrastructure buildout thesis intact. Gold (NEM +14.8% 1W) is the strongest defense play. Semis are mixed: MRVL +9.7% 1W leading, TSM +4.0% 1W recovering, NVDA +3.6% 1W solid, but ARM -3.7% 1W giving back. Memory (SNDK +16.3% 1W, WDC +7.9% 1W) is surprisingly strong — AI storage supercycle narrative. Defense (RTX flat) not getting much bid yet despite Iran headlines. Energy (LNG -3.5% 1W) lagging despite oil spike — thesis not playing out quickly. INTC +14.2% 1W is an outlier — Apollo $14.2B deal catalyst. **Positions:** 9 open positions, ~36% deployed, ~64% cash. Working: MRVL +7.3% (NVIDIA $2B deal thesis intact, +9.7% 1W showing institutional accumulation), NEM +0.2% unrealized but +14.8% 1W strong (gold dual tailwind intact), ARM +3.5% (IBM deal confirms AI chip pivot thesis), TSEM +15.1% (parabolic but still near 52W high -0.6%). At risk: LNG flat (+0.0%) but -3.5% 1W — Iran war not translating to stock price; thesis slow; RTX flat — defense thesis not paying off yet, stock just matching SPY. TSM -0.7% reporting April 16 — hold. MU -0.4% slight lag. GS +0.3% into earnings Monday. ASML +0.6% with earnings April 15 and China restriction headwinds ongoing — Barron's specifically calls ASML most at risk from new House bill. Key decision points: (1) GS earnings Monday — hold small 4-share position; risk/reward reasonable. (2) ASML earnings April 15 — the new House bill specifically targeting ASML is a material risk; Billionaire Steve Cohen buying is bullish but legislative headwind is real. (3) TSM April 16 — strong setup, Cohen also buying. (4) LNG underperforming despite oil spike — reconsider. (5) Optical networking pullback opportunity — CIEN still near highs, not yet a pullback to add. New opportunities: INTC +14.2% 1W on Apollo deal is interesting but fwd P/E 50.8x with -4% revenue growth is not compelling fundamentally. SNDK +16.3% 1W, fwd P/E 8.0x, WDC +7.9% — memory storage thesis gaining momentum; SNDK at fwd P/E 8x with +61% revenue growth is compelling. GLW +9.3% 1W on Meta $6B AI fiber deal — optical networking adjacency, fwd P/E 37.9x not cheap but catalyst is fresh. NKE -15.1% 1W, -24.6% 1M — potential short? fwd P/E 22.6x, 0% revenue growth, -43.9% from 52W high, vol ratio 2.67x (unusual volume on selloff). AXON -8.4% 1W. Actions: (a) TRIM LNG — flat despite Iran thesis, -3.5% 1W, oil spike not translating; sell 3 shares to reduce risk while keeping small exposure for further escalation. (b) Add SNDK — memory storage AI supercycle, fwd P/E 8x on +61% rev growth, +16.3% 1W momentum, WDC AI storage narrative; small position 5 shares. (c) Consider SHORT NKE — -15.1% 1W, -24.6% 1M, -43.9% from 52W high, 0% revenue growth, vol ratio 2.67x (institutional distribution), fwd P/E 22.6x overvalued for zero-growth brand. However, bounces happen and I already have some shorts in recent history. NKE is at ~$44, very beaten up — the risk/reward on shorting something already -44% from highs is asymmetric vs mean reversion. Pass on short. (d) Hold ASML into earnings April 15 — Cohen buying is bullish signal, but China bill is specific headwind. Will hold 5 shares (small position, ~6.6% weight) and reassess post-earnings. (e) Hold GS into Monday earnings — small 4-share position, thesis intact on capital markets reactivation. Cash management: After selling 3 LNG (~$843) and buying 5 SNDK (~$3,508), net cash decreases by ~$2,665. Cash goes from $64,244 to ~$61,579. Still ~60% cash, justified as: major binary earnings events this week (GS Monday, ASML/MS/PNC Tuesday, TSM/NFLX/ABT/SCHW Wednesday), Iran macro uncertainty, and positioning for post-earnings deployment.

**Macro:** Market regime is risk-on with benchmarks surging: SPY +3.9% 1W, QQQ +4.6% 1W, SMH +5.8% 1W — a strong week driven by Iran cease-fire hopes (Trump offering 'olive branch' per IBD), strong jobs data, and broad AI infrastructure momentum. Oil at $110 with Strait of Hormuz closure fears creates cross-current, but equity markets are rallying on diplomatic breakthrough hopes. Bond yields rising (strong jobs pushes back Fed cuts) remains a headwind for rate-sensitive growth but not derailing the AI/semi rally. **Sectors:** Semiconductors leading (SMH +5.8% 1W) — MRVL +15.4% 1W is extraordinary, INTC +17.7% 1W on Apollo deal, AMD +9.0% 1W, NVDA +6.0% 1W. Memory storage exploding: SNDK +17.7% 1W, WDC +10.5% 1W. Optical networking (LITE +9.9%, CIEN +8.1%) continuing higher. Defense strong: RTX +4.6%, LMT +3.6%. Gold (NEM +10.5%) remains dual-tailwind play. Banks rallying into earnings: GS +7.9% 1W, C +9.3% 1W, JPM +4.5% 1W. Energy lagging (CVX -5.8%, XOM -4.5%, LNG -4.3%) as Iran cease-fire hopes reduce oil premium. **Positions:** Portfolio performing well — MRVL +9.7% unrealized is the star, TSEM +10.6%, MU +2.7%, SNDK +3.3%, ARM +3.2%, NVDA +1.9%. At risk: LNG -4.3% 1W as Iran de-escalation thesis hurts energy; NEM -0.8% unrealized but still +10.5% 1W strong. ASML flat to slightly negative with earnings April 15 and China export bill headwind. GS EARNINGS ALERT: GS reports April 13 (today/tomorrow). Holding 4 shares at +0.7% unrealized. BofA says banks set for in-line to better quarter — capital markets reactivation thesis intact, GS upgraded Netflix to Buy this week showing active dealmaking. Will HOLD into earnings — small 4-share position (~3.4% weight) limits binary risk, thesis intact on capital markets rebound. Key decisions: (1) LNG is now a problem — Iran cease-fire hopes are directly undermining the LNG war premium thesis, stock -4.3% 1W while energy sector broadly weak; CLOSE LNG. (2) TSEM at +10.6% unrealized and 39.7x fwd P/E with only +14% revenue growth — valuation stretched, GlobalFoundries patent suit risk, stock +10.6% 1W; TRIM to take profits. (3) MRVL is exceptional — +15.4% 1W, +44.7% 1M, new Buy initiation from Insider Monkey, NVIDIA $2B deal thesis, fwd P/E 20.3x on 22% growth is reasonable; HOLD and consider adding. (4) WDC +10.5% 1W — same AI storage thesis as SNDK; could add WDC as complement. (5) MPWR +12.3% 1W, Citi bullish, fwd P/E 45.7x on 21% growth — interesting but expensive. (6) AMD +9.0% 1W with Citi 30-day positive catalyst watch, fwd P/E 20.4x on 34% growth — compelling for swing trade. (7) UNH +8.6% 1W on Medicare rate boost — fwd P/E 14x on 12% growth with fresh FDA catalyst; interesting defensive/health play. (8) C +9.3% 1W reports April 14 — could add small pre-earnings position given BofA 'in-line to better quarter' call. (9) ASML earnings April 15 — holding 5 shares, small position; China export bill is headwind but Cohen buying bullish; HOLD small position. Actions: SELL LNG (thesis broken by Iran cease-fire), TRIM TSEM (take profits on 10.6% gain at stretched valuation), BUY AMD (Citi catalyst watch + strong momentum + reasonable valuation), BUY WDC (AI storage supercycle complement to SNDK, +10.5% 1W, fwd P/E 22.3x). **Cash:** Reducing cash from ~61% toward ~50% by deploying into high-conviction setups post-Iran de-escalation signal; maintaining dry powder for post-earnings deployment this week (JPM/C/JNJ April 14, ASML/MS/PNC April 15, TSM/NFLX April 16).
